expectations.text: Output contains all 6 rounds (Round 1 through Round 6)
expectations.evidence: All 6 rounds present: Round 1 Optimizer Proposes (line 67), Round 2 Frontline Operator Challenges (line 99), Round 3 Optimizer Defends (line 117), Round 4 Frontline Operator Stress-Tests (line 135), Round 5 Optimizer Final Stand (line 147), Round 6 COO Decides (line 168)

expectations.text: Optimizer, Frontline Operator, and COO personas all appear and are distinct
expectations.evidence: Optimizer proposes data-driven outsourcing with cost math; Frontline Operator challenges with specific scenarios (knowledge transfer, attrition, valley of death); COO synthesizes with pilot-first recommendation

expectations.text: Every challenge in Round 2 has a severity rating (critical/significant/minor)
expectations.evidence: 7 challenges with severity: critical (1,2), significant (3,4,5,7), minor (6)
Categories present: execution-risk, people-impact, transition-cost, measurement, quality-risk, dependency

expectations.text: Round 4 references specific mitigations from Round 3, not just repeating Round 2
expectations.evidence: Round 4 directly attacks Round 3 mitigations: challenges the 'start narrow' runbook approach, the '50% allocation' for senior agents, the temporary contractor proposal, and the hidden cost math

expectations.text: Round 6 contains one of the 5 valid recommendations
A CEO Summary section exists that is standalone and uses business language
expectations.evidence: CEO Summary at line 12-14, standalone with budget, headcount, and timeline details in business language

expectations.text: Metrics table present with all 8 metrics filled in
expectations.evidence: All 8 metrics present: challengesRaised=7, stressTestsRaised=4, challengesSurvived=2, challengesConceded=3, mitigationsHeld=2, mitigationsBroken=2, proposalModified=yes, confidenceScore=7

expectations.text: People impact summary with headcount changes, roles affected, transition support, attrition risk
expectations.evidence: People impact summary at lines 47-50 with all 4 required sections: Headcount (+1), Roles affected (5 agents shift), Transition support (briefing, communication), Attrition risk (moderate, with mitigations)

expectations.text: Round 5 includes fallback plans for surviving challenges
expectations.evidence: Round 5 includes fallback plans: routing layer with 10% miscategorization pause gate, hire team lead FIRST, drop contractor, quality gates before expansion
